stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers and llama.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text-generation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics such as transformers, text-generation, and Llama.
The content points to the availability and community interest in this specific Meta model variant.

stories.title: SpaceX to acquire AI coding platform Cursor for $60 billion
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly acquiring the AI coding platform Cursor.
The acquisition is valued at a massive $60 billion.
The move signals a major strategic push by SpaceX into advanced AI development and coding capabilities.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags suggest relevance to transformers, safetensors, and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
This preview suggests a significant boost in local AI inference speed on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device, emphasizing privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Snap is finally about to ship AR glasses — and they cost a fortune
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Snap is launching its first public augmented reality (AR) glasses, named 'Specs'.
The device is positioned as a 'wearable computer' and carries a high price tag of $2,195.
Preorders are available with a $200 deposit, with shipments expected in the US and UK this fall.

stories.title: EvolveNav: Proactive Preflection and Self-Evolving Memory for Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation
stories.author: Hao Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EvolveNav, a framework for Zero-Shot Object-Goal Navigation (ZS-OGN) that allows agents to operate without prior training.
Addresses the limitation of current methods (reliance on static priors) by enabling continuous, test-time improvement.
Proposes an agentic rule memory system to extract actionable knowledge, facilitating self-evolution of the navigation agent.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM) to address the tension between simulation fidelity and computational cost in world models.
LoopWM utilizes a parameter-shared transformer block to iteratively refine latent environment states.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency compared to conventional world modeling approaches.

stories.title: A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models
stories.author: Nicola Franco
bullet_points: The study performs a red-team evaluation of Anthropic's frontier LLMs, Fable 5 and Opus 4.8.
It tests adversarial robustness against four families of automated jailbreak attacks across 7,826 harmful intents.
The methodology uses the HackAgent framework and employs a panel of three judge models for adjudication.

stories.title: The Stanford EDGAR Filings Dataset: Reconstructing U.S. Corporate and Financial Disclosures into Layout-Faithful and Token-Efficient Pretraining Data
stories.author: Kay Giesecke
bullet_points: Introduces the Stanford EDGAR Filings Dataset (SEFD), an open-source reconstruction of SEC filings.
Addresses the scarcity of high-quality, clean, long-context documents needed for training Large Language Models (LLMs).
Formats the data into layout-faithful MultiMarkdown specifically for financial language model pretraining.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM), a novel looped architecture for world modeling.
Addresses the trade-off between simulation fidelity and computational cost in current world models.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency by iteratively refining latent states using a parameter-shared transformer block.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM), a novel architecture addressing the computational cost of long-horizon world simulations.
LoopWM iteratively refines latent environment states using a parameter-shared transformer block.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency compared to conventional world modeling approaches.

stories.title: A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models
stories.author: Nicola Franco
bullet_points: The study performs a red-teaming analysis on Anthropic's frontier LLMs (Fable 5 and Opus 4.8) to test adversarial robustness.
It evaluates the models against four families of automated jailbreak attacks across 7,826 harmful intents.
The methodology uses the HackAgent framework and employs a panel of three judge models for independent adjudication of attack successes.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and reduced memory usage for models.
The updated engine also contributes to higher quality outputs from the language models.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: The next humanoid robot might not look human at all
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: The concept of humanoid robots is evolving beyond human-like aesthetics.
Genesis AI's new robot, Eno, exemplifies a non-traditional design, potentially featuring a wheeled base.
The article highlights a shift in industry thinking: humanoid function does not necessitate human appearance.

stories.title: All the latest news on Android 17, Wear OS 7, and Android XR
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Android 17 introduces multitasking features like floating 'Bubble' app windows and a 50/50 split gaming mode for foldables.
Wear OS 7 focuses on improving smartwatches with Live Updates and enhanced battery life.
The ecosystem is advancing with preparations for new Android XR smart glasses.

stories.title: Google launches Wear OS 7 with Live Updates and a battery life boost
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google has released Wear OS 7, starting with the Pixel Watch 2, 3, and 4.
The key new feature is 'Live Updates,' which tracks real-time events on the smartwatch.
Live Updates will sync across Wear OS devices, displaying things like sports scores or delivery statuses.

stories.title: Qualcomm’s latest chip hints that more powerful smart glasses could be on the way
stories.author: Victoria Song
bullet_points: Qualcomm is developing the Snapdragon Reality Elite chip to power next-generation XR devices.
The chip aims to enable more powerful smart glasses, indicating progress in a nascent market.
The announcement was made at the Augmented World Expo, with early hands-on demos available.

stories.title: The Google / Xreal Aura XR glasses are now available to preorder
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: The Google/Xreal Aura XR glasses are now available for preorder.
The device is priced starting at $99 and is positioned as the second Android XR device.
A full launch across major markets (US, UK, Japan, etc.) is anticipated this Fall.

stories.title: Anthropic "pauses" token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has temporarily halted the implementation of token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK.
The planned billing change was set to significantly increase operational costs for power users.
This pause suggests a responsiveness to user feedback or a strategic adjustment in pricing models for AI agents.

stories.title: Pentagon boasts of using AI to write reports mandated by Congress
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The Pentagon is reportedly using AI, specifically generative AI, to draft reports required by Congress.
The Department of Defense claims that 1.5 million personnel are currently utilizing generative AI tools.
The story highlights the integration and adoption of AI within a major government/defense entity.

stories.title: Trump admin abandons fight against wind energy as clean energy output surges
stories.author: Aman Azhar, Inside Climate News
bullet_points: The Trump administration's attempts to impede wind and solar energy have reportedly failed.
Legal challenges have been instrumental in undermining the administration's efforts against clean energy.
Clean energy output is noted to be surging despite regulatory opposition.

stories.title: Visual Verification Enables Inference-time Steering and Autonomous Policy Improvement
stories.author: Dhruv Shah
bullet_points: Introduces VERITAS, a generator-verifier framework designed for generalist robot policies.
Enables inference-time policy steering and self-improvement by pairing a pre-trained policy (generator) with a gradient-free visual verifier.
Addresses the need for real-world robots to learn and improve continuously from experiential feedback.

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: Introduces ReproRepo, a new framework designed to scale reproducibility audits in AI research.
Addresses the major bottleneck in current reproducibility benchmarks: the reliance on substantial manual effort for data curation.
Leverages human-raised GitHub issues as a form of natural supervision for realistic reproduction evaluations.

stories.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
stories.author: Xenofon Koutsoukos
bullet_points: The research focuses on training autonomous cyber-defense agents using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to combat sophisticated cyber-attacks.
It introduces a neurosymbolic approach, integrating behavior trees with learning-enabled components (LECs) for reasoning and adaptation.
The core challenge addressed is handling the partially observable nature of the system, specifically the unknown actions of the 'red agent' (attacker).

stories.title: RubricsTree: Scalable and Evolving Open-Ended Evaluation of Personal Health Agents across Health Memory and Medical Skills
stories.author: Ahmed A. Metwally
bullet_points: Introduces RubricsTree, a new framework to address the bottleneck in evaluating LLM-powered personal health agents.
The framework aims to overcome the limitations of traditional evaluation methods (costly physician annotation vs. subjective LLM-as-a-judge).
Focuses on scalable and evolving open-ended evaluation across health memory and medical skills for clinical deployment.

stories.title: DRFLOW: A Deep Research Benchmark for Personalized Workflow Prediction
stories.author: Issam H. Laradji
bullet_points: Introduces DRFLOW, a new benchmark specifically designed for Personalized Workflow Prediction in Deep Research (DR) systems.
Addresses a gap in current DR research, which often focuses on generating summaries rather than identifying concrete, sequential action-step workflows.
Highlights the practical enterprise need for agents to map out complex processes (e.g., 'How do I request new headcount?') rather than just summarizing policies.

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: Introduces ReproRepo, a new framework designed to scale the evaluation of research reproducibility.
Addresses the major limitation of existing reproducibility benchmarks: reliance on extensive manual effort for data curation.
Leverages naturally occurring human-raised GitHub issues as a scalable form of supervision for testing reproduction capabilities.

stories.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
stories.author: Xenofon Koutsoukos
bullet_points: The research focuses on developing autonomous cyber-defense agents using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to combat sophisticated cyber-attacks.
The agents utilize a neurosymbolic approach, integrating learning-enabled components (LECs) with behavior trees for reasoning and adaptation.
A key challenge addressed is the partial observability of the system, where the attacker's actions are not fully known to the defense agent.

stories.title: Multi-Source Cybersecurity Logs: An ATT&CK-Labeled Dataset and SLM Evaluation
stories.author: Md Rayhanur Rahman
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of detecting multi-stage cyberattacks by correlating events across system, network, and browser logs.
A new dataset, ATLAS, is introduced to provide labeled, multi-source data necessary for training machine learning models for cybersecurity.
The study highlights a gap in existing public datasets, which often focus on only one or two of the required log sources (network, host, or browser).

stories.title: Kolmogorov Regression for Robust Diffusion Policies
stories.author: Lekan Molu
bullet_points: Addresses temporal drift in finite-dimensional diffusion policies, a limitation in long-horizon physical deployments.
Introduces a backward Kolmogorov equation to lift diffusion policies into a Cameron-Martin space.
Replaces stochastic score matching with a deterministic boundary-value Partial Differential Equation (PDE) problem.

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: Introduces ReproRepo, a new framework designed to scale the evaluation of research reproducibility.
It addresses the scalability issue in current reproducibility benchmarks by using human-raised GitHub issues for supervision.
The framework aims to make the auditing of research results from code repositories more realistic and less manually intensive.

stories.title: RubricsTree: Scalable and Evolving Open-Ended Evaluation of Personal Health Agents across Health Memory and Medical Skills
stories.author: Ahmed A. Metwally
bullet_points: Introduces RubricsTree, a new scalable evaluation framework for personal health agents.
Addresses the critical bottleneck of open-ended evaluation in healthcare AI, contrasting costly human annotation with subjective LLM-as-a-judge methods.
Focuses on improving the reliability and scalability of assessing LLM-empowered agents that integrate user health metrics for healthcare access.

stories.title: Learning from the Self-future: On-policy Self-distillation for dLLMs
stories.author: Shiwei Liu
bullet_points: Introduces d-OPSD, the first On-policy Self-distillation (OPSD) framework specifically designed for diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation: existing OPSD methods are autoregressive-centric and conflict with the arbitrary-order generation nature of dLLMs.
The research focuses on adapting distillation techniques to enhance the capabilities of dLLMs post-training.

stories.title: The Measurement Gap in the Automation of EU Law: Benchmarking Doctrinal Legal Reasoning under the EU AI Act
stories.author: Michèle Finck
bullet_points: Current LLMs can produce legally competent text, but existing benchmarks fail to test core doctrinal legal reasoning.
The research identifies a 'measurement gap' in evaluating AI's ability to perform complex legal interpretation, not just basic tasks.
This gap is critical because the EU AI Act mandates 'appropriate accuracy' for high-risk AI in judicial settings.

stories.title: Show HN: High-Res Neural Cellular Automata
stories.author: esychology
bullet_points: Researchers have developed Neural Cellular Automata (CAs) capable of generating patterns at high definition (HD) resolution in real-time.
The key innovation involves transforming each CA cell into a Neural Field, enabling higher fidelity simulations.
Demos showcase applications including pattern growth/healing, synthesizing regenerative PBR textures, and creating 3D textures.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: roman.pt via hoistbypetard
bullet_points: The story discusses a security vulnerability or 'backdoor' found within a LinkedIn job offer context.
This suggests a potential risk related to phishing, social engineering, or platform security on LinkedIn.
The nature of the vulnerability is a significant security concern, placing it in the moderate-to-high relevance category.

stories.title: Why the Fable 5 Crisis Proves Your AI Context Layer Can't Live Inside the Model
stories.author: jon_at_backboardio
bullet_points: The article discusses the 'Fable 5 Crisis' as a case study.
It argues against embedding AI context layers directly within the core model.
The central theme advocates for separating intelligence (model) from memory (context layer).

stories.title: Your AI Provider Is a Single Point of Failure
stories.author: maishsk
bullet_points: The story addresses the critical issue of AI provider dependency and single points of failure.
It references regulatory action, specifically a letter from the U.S. Commerce Department to Anthropic.
The content suggests an emerging theme of governmental oversight impacting major AI companies.

stories.title: The $0 Bug That Cost Us $1,800 in API Calls
stories.author: arpitstack
bullet_points: A developer experienced a massive, unexpected spike in OpenAI API costs ($620 to $2,480 in 23 days).
The cost increase occurred without any corresponding new feature deployments, suggesting a hidden inefficiency or bug.
The story highlights the financial risks and operational blind spots associated with scaling LLM usage.

stories.title: Startup Security Guide & LLM CISO
stories.author: denniskim
bullet_points: Provides an open-source security guide tailored for startups.
Includes a compliance checklist to aid early-stage company governance.
Features an LLM-based virtual CISO persona for accessible security advice.
